run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN (egress), third consecutive day. Every WebFetch was blocked by an organization egress-policy 403 on the CONNECT tunnel - confirmed against AusTender, buy.nsw, VendorPanel and a neutral control (en.wikipedia.org), and reproduced with a direct curl through the proxy (curl: (56) CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403). Per /root/.ccr/README.md a blanket 403 is a policy denial that must not be retried or routed around, so no live tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run. Only WebSearch (indexed result snippets, not live pages) was available, so every field below is snippet-derived and must be re-verified once egress is restored. Despite that, this run captured TWO net-new Commonwealth AusTender opportunities, both genuinely recent (published 29-30 June 2026, i.e. the last 48 hours) and currently open, with real ATM IDs and deep links: OPC-2026-03 (Office of Parliamentary Counsel - print-on-demand service for the Federal Register of Legislation, closing 29-Jul-2026, full deliverables captured) and ATM_2026_6066 (DCCEEW Australian Bushland Program - RFI/EOI for strategic property purchasing partners, closing 3-Aug-2026). One net-new source was discovered: James Cook University Strategic Procurement (publishes via VendorPanel). Tier 1 (all nine jurisdictions), Tier 2 (councils/GBE/university/health) and the mandatory Tier 3 (private sector + new-source discovery) were all attempted via WebSearch. Several AusTender IDs that surfaced in the index were verified as already CLOSED or stale (ATM26/14 closed 30-Jun-2026; DPS26000776 closed 13-Apr-2026; ATM_2026_6551 closed 8-Jun-2026) and were excluded rather than presented as open. SAP Ariba Discovery and corporate Tier-3 buyer portals remain registration/login-gated with no publicly-visible AU RFx capturable without an authenticated session - recorded as portals only, never fabricated as tenders.
gaps: EGRESS POLICY BLOCK (dominant gap, unchanged from 2026-06-29 and 2026-06-30 - now a 3-day pattern): the session egress proxy returned HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel for ALL destinations, confirmed for tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw.gov.au and vendorpanel.com.au plus a neutral control (en.wikipedia.org), and reproduced with a direct curl (exit 56, 'CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403'). The proxy status endpoint shows recentRelayFailures empty. Per /root/.ccr/README.md this is an organization egress-policy denial and was not retried or routed around. No tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run. ACTION REQUIRED: add AU government/buyer domains to the session egress allowlist (e.g. *.gov.au, vendorpanel.com.au, qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au, *.ariba.com, *.coupahost.com) so WebFetch can read full deliverables/requirements/documents again.

Both captured opportunities are SNIPPET-DERIVED from public WebSearch summaries of the AusTender detail pages, NOT from a direct page fetch. For OPC-2026-03 the title, scope, deliverables, publish/closing dates, contract term and contact were extractable; for ATM_2026_6066 the title, agency, type, category code, publish and closing dates were extractable. For BOTH, the tender documents, evaluation criteria, value, eligibility and mandatory conditions could not be captured (fetch blocked) - these need a verification/detail pass once egress is restored.

Excluded as already CLOSED (surfaced in the AusTender index as 'Current ATM' pages but verified closed via snippet): ATM26/14 (National Memorial for Victims and Survivors of Institutional Child Sexual Abuse - Landscape Head Contractor, National Capital Authority, closed 30-Jun-2026 2:00pm ACT); DPS26000776 (Certification of Safety Equipment at Australian Parliament House, closed 13-Apr-2026); ATM_2026_6551 (Energy Pipeline Data Services, DCCEEW, closed 8-Jun-2026). The search index is returning stale 'current' pages, so closing dates from snippets are unreliable and were cross-checked before inclusion.

ATM_2026_6026 (DCCEEW - 'National Solar Panel Recycling Pilot', with a parallel RFI_ATM_2026_6026) surfaced as a Current ATM but the snippet says the contract was expected to commence ~May 2026, implying the approach to market most likely already closed/awarded. Could not confirm it is open - deferred rather than presented as a current opportunity.
ATM_2026_5994 surfaced but the snippet gave no title/agency/closing date (only 'execute June 2026, up to 12-month term depending on modules awarded') - not enough to capture honestly; deferred.

Tenders WA: PROPTA26006 surfaced as an open tender (id 68624) but no title/agency/closing date was extractable from search (JavaScript-rendered); a scheduled outage 1-Jul-2026 17:00-19:00 was noted. Deferred.

Tenders SA / Buying4SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL and Tenders ACT: no individual current opportunity was extractable via search this run (only portal/landing pages indexed). Deferred to a future run with fetch access.

buy.nsw / NSW eTendering and Buying for Victoria: only previously-known items reappeared in snippets (e.g. VIC DOT43290 Belmore Rd POS, already captured 2026-06-29) - no net-new NSW or VIC opportunity captured this run.

Tier 2 councils (VendorPanel public board), universities and GBEs (Sydney/Melbourne/SA Water): individual current opportunities could not be verified because the VendorPanel public board and every buyer domain were unreachable via WebFetch (403). JCU's procurement page was discovered (new source) but its live VendorPanel opportunity list requires registration and could not be read.

Tier 3 (mandatory): SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery confirmed (via SAP help docs) to require a free seller account to view/respond, with public posting dependent on buyer config - no publicly-visible AU RFx capturable without login. EstimateOne (construction subcontractor packages) requires registration. Corporate buyer portals (BHP/Rio/Fortescue/Woodside on Ariba; Qantas/Westpac on Coupa; banks on SAP Business Network; Woolworths/Coles) remain registration- or invitation-gated. Portals retained in the known source set; no corporate opportunity fabricated. Invitation-only consultancy RFPs remain out of scope.
recommended_seed_additions: James Cook University (JCU) - Strategic Procurement, publishes most tenders via the VendorPanel e-Tendering portal (free registration); useful as a seed to find sibling universities on VendorPanel. Check weekly. (Added to discovered_sources this run.)

Office of Parliamentary Counsel (OPC) - Commonwealth buyer publishing ATMs on AusTender under the 'OPC-' prefix (e.g. OPC-2026-02 printing/distribution, OPC-2026-03 print-on-demand, and a managed-SOC procurement reported separately); add to the agency sweep list.

INFRASTRUCTURE (repeat from 2026-06-29 and 2026-06-30, now urgent - 3-day outage): add AU government/buyer domains to the session egress allowlist so WebFetch can open detail pages and documents again. Without this the routine can only ever capture snippet-level data and cannot extract deliverables, documents, evaluation criteria or values.

tenders.title: Print on Demand Service for the Federal Register of Legislation
tenders.category: Printing / print-on-demand and e-commerce order fulfilment services
tenders.description: Open approach to market by the Office of Parliamentary Counsel for a print-on-demand service to fulfil orders placed by clients on the Federal Register of Legislation, including an online ordering/shopping-cart and secure payment capability integrated with the Register website.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provide and operate a print-on-demand service that fulfils orders made by clients on the Federal Register of Legislation. The service provider must supply a website with a shopping-cart function that lets a client order multiple separate items in one transaction and securely accept payment, with a seamless transition from the Federal Register of Legislation website to the provider's ordering site, and print and deliver the ordered legislative material on demand. The provider must transition in from 1 October 2026 so services are operational by the expiry of the existing arrangement.
key_deliverables: Print-on-demand printing and fulfilment of legislative material ordered via the Federal Register of Legislation
A website with a shopping-cart that supports ordering multiple separate items in a single transaction
Secure online payment acceptance for orders
Seamless hand-off from the Federal Register of Legislation website to the provider's ordering/shopping-cart site
Transition-in and implementation so services commence by the expiry of the existing arrangement
tenders.scope_of_work: The Service Provider is to provide a print-on-demand service to fulfil orders made by clients on the Federal Register of Legislation. This includes a website that provides a shopping cart function enabling the ordering of multiple separate items in one transaction and that can securely accept payment for the order, with the client able to transition seamlessly from the Federal Register of Legislation website to the Service Provider's website/shopping cart during the ordering process. Transition-in activities should commence on 1 October 2026 so that services are able to commence by the expiry of the existing arrangement.
tenders.contract_term: Approximately 3 years (9 November 2026 to 8 November 2029), with transition-in from 1 October 2026
tenders.submission_method: Lodge electronically via AusTender (www.tenders.gov.au) before the ATM close date and time
tenders.delivery_location: Australia (national; service supports the Federal Register of Legislation)
tenders.detail_source: WebSearch indexed snippet of the AusTender ATM OPC-2026-03 detail page (direct fetch blocked by egress 403)
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via WebSearch of the AusTender Current ATM list; buyer-originated Commonwealth notice published on AusTender by the Office of Parliamentary Counsel. Net-new for this routine and published in the last 48 hours (30-Jun-2026).
tenders.notes: Title, scope/deliverables, publish date (30-Jun-2026), closing date (29-Jul-2026), contract term (9-Nov-2026 to 8-Nov-2029, transition from 1-Oct-2026) and contact (Anne Sadleir, Tenders@opc.gov.au) are from the AusTender snippet. Closing TIME, tender documents, evaluation criteria, value, eligibility and mandatory conditions could not be captured because the live page/documents were unreachable (egress 403). Verify against the live notice when fetch access is restored. OPC also runs OPC-2026-02 (printing & distribution of Bills, already known) - distinct opportunity.

tenders.title: Request for Information - Australian Bushland Program Strategic Property Purchases
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW) - Australian Bushland Program
tenders.description: Request for Information / expression of interest by the Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water, on behalf of the Australian Bushland Program, inviting potential Purchasing Partners to express interest in supporting strategic property purchases.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Identify and engage potential Purchasing Partners able to support the Australian Bushland Program's strategic property purchases. As an RFI / expression of interest, respondents would set out their capability and interest in acquiring or helping acquire strategic bushland/conservation properties on behalf of the program; the precise scope of deliverables would follow in any subsequent approach to market.
tenders.scope_of_work: The Australian Bushland Program, represented by DCCEEW, is inviting expressions of interest from potential Purchasing Partners for strategic property purchases. (Headline-level - the detailed scope and obligations are in the RFI documents, which were not accessible this run.)
tenders.eligibility: Open to potential Purchasing Partners (capability/interest in strategic property purchasing for the Australian Bushland Program)
tenders.submission_method: Lodge electronically via AusTender (www.tenders.gov.au) before the ATM close date and time
tenders.detail_source: WebSearch indexed snippet of the AusTender ATM ATM_2026_6066 detail page (direct fetch blocked by egress 403)
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via WebSearch of the AusTender Current ATM list; buyer-originated Commonwealth notice published on AusTender by DCCEEW for the Australian Bushland Program. Net-new for this routine and published in the last 48 hours (29-Jun-2026).
tenders.notes: HEADLINE-LEVEL (pending detail). Title, buyer, ATM type (Expression of Interest / RFI), category code (77100000 Environmental management), publish date (29-Jun-2026) and closing date/time (3-Aug-2026 2:00pm ACT) are from the AusTender snippet. The RFI documents, value, eligibility detail, evaluation criteria and contact could not be captured because the live page/documents were unreachable (egress 403). Needs a full deliverables/detail pass when fetch access is restored.

discovered_sources.source_name: James Cook University (JCU) - Strategic Procurement (VendorPanel e-Tendering)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for Australian university procurement portals surfaced JCU's Strategic Procurement page, which states JCU issues most of its tenders (RFQ/RFP/RFT) through the VendorPanel e-Tendering portal, with lower-value items via a JCU tenders mailbox.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated public-university procurement source (Queensland). Tenders are published on VendorPanel (free registration to download documents and respond); contact tenders@jcu.edu.au. Useful as a seed to detect sibling universities/TAFEs that publish on VendorPanel. Live opportunity list requires registration and could not be read this run (egress 403).
